name: Consumer Protection Act, 2019
description: Comprehensive consumer protection law covering consumer rights, remedies, and consumer disputes.

sections.title: Definitions
sections.content: Key definitions including consumer, consumer dispute, defect, unfair trade practice, etc.

subsections.title: Consumer
subsections.content: A consumer is any natural person who buys any goods or avails of any service for a consideration. The money paid for goods or service may be paid by any mode of consideration.

subsections.title: Unfair trade practice
subsections.content: Any practice which for the purpose of promoting the sale, use or supply of any goods or services, adopts any unfair method or unfair or deceptive practice.

sections.title: Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission
sections.content: Establishment of District, State, and National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commissions to hear and dispose of consumer complaints.
explanations: District Commission: Complaints up to Rs. 1 crore
State Commission: Appeals and cases above Rs. 1 crore but up to Rs. 10 crores
National Commission: Appeals and cases above Rs. 10 crores

sections.title: Consumer complaint
sections.content: A consumer shall make a complaint in writing to the District Commission within two years from the date on which the cause of action has arisen.

sections.title: Grounds of complaint
sections.content: A complaint may be filed on grounds that goods or services were not as agreed, or have defects, or due to unfair trade practices.

subsections.title: Complaint grounds
subsections.content:
(a) Goods are defective or services are deficient
(b) Goods are hazardous or services cause injury
(c) Consumer is charged unfairly
(d) Misleading advertisements
(e) Unfair or restrictive trade practices

sections.title: Relief
sections.content: In the case of a complaint about defective goods or deficient services, the consumer may seek: removal or replacement of goods, refund with interest, compensation for loss or injury, cost of proceedings.

subsections.title: Remedies available
subsections.content:
(a) Removal of defect by repair
(b) Replacement of goods
(c) Refund of the price
(d) Compensation
(e) Costs of the complaint

sections.title: Recovery of compensation
sections.content: Where a District Commission grants any relief, the same shall be complied within 30 days from the date of order, failing which the consumer may recover the amount with interest at 12% per annum.

procedures.name: Filing Consumer Complaint
steps: Prepare written complaint with supporting documents
File complaint within 2 years of cause of action
Pay required fee as per jurisdiction
Present case with evidence and witnesses
